text_blocks.snippet: Couples counselling in Vancouver involves working with a neutral, trained professional—typically a Registered Clinical Counsellor (RCC) or Registered Psychologist—to improve communication, resolve conflicts, and strengthen emotional intimacy. In a city with high cost-of-living stressors, therapists often use evidence-based approaches like Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) and The Gottman Method to help couples break negative interaction patterns.

list.snippet: Initial Consultation & Assessment: Many Vancouver clinics offer a free 15–20 minute consultation to ensure a good "fit". The first 1-3 sessions usually involve a "relationship inventory" to map out strengths, challenges, and goals.
Structure of Sessions: Sessions are commonly 50 minutes to 90 minutes long, focusing on identifying the "dance" or cycle of conflict rather than blaming one partner.
EFT (Emotionally Focused Therapy): Focuses on emotional needs and attachment, helping partners feel secure and reconnected.
Gottman Method: Research-based approach focusing on building communication tools, managing conflict, and creating shared meaning.
Online and In-Person: Many firms (e.g., Wellspring Counselling, Clearheart Counselling) offer both in-person (e.g., South Granville, Kitsilano) and secure video sessions across BC.
Duration: While some couples see results in 6–12 sessions, others may engage in longer-term, 10+ sessions, depending on the complexity of the issues (e.g., infidelity, deep-rooted disconnection).

text_blocks.snippet: Couples in Vancouver often seek therapy for:

list.snippet: Rebuilding Trust: Recovery from infidelity or betrayal.
Life Transitions: Navigating marriage, new parenthood, or career pressure.
Intimacy Issues: Emotional or physical distance.
Rates: Typical fees for a Registered Clinical Counsellor (RCC) in Vancouver range from $130 to $200+ per 50-minute session.
Lower-Cost Options: Intern or student counsellors may offer sessions between $40–$80.
Insurance: Many extended health plans in BC cover Registered Clinical Counsellors (RCCs), but MSP does not.

text_blocks.snippet: How to Find a Counsellor in Vancouver

Look for specialists registered with the BC Association of Clinical Counsellors (BCACC) or Canadian Certified Counsellors (CCC).
Popular options include:

Couples therapy works best when both partners are committed to the process, though some therapists, such as those at Sana Counselling, also work with individuals seeking to change their own relationship patterns.
